insights.recommendation: Implement key actions to improve acceptance, focusing on reducing time-to-offer and addressing role scope clarity.

evidence.key_insight: Current 39.7% acceptance rate is significantly below the industry benchmark of 70-80% for senior roles, with time-to-offer and role clarity issues identified.

alternative.trade_off: Lower cost but reduced understanding of complex industry benchmarks.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer outcomes summary (57 offers made, 200 candidates total). Offers accepted: 34 (59.6% acceptance rate). Offers declined: 23 (40.4% decline rate). Overall pipeline conversion: 34 hires from 200 candidates = 17% end-to-end conversion. Industry benchmark for offer acceptance: 70-80% for senior engineering roles. Current 59.6% acceptance rate is 10-20 points below benchmark — significant gap requiring action. Each declined offer represents ~60 hours of interview time wasted across panels.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"offer_acceptance_summary\"},\"id\":\"offer-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Actions to improve offer acceptance rate from 59.6% to benchmark 75%+. (1) Address culture concerns: add team lunch/coffee with potential teammates before offer stage. (2) Reduce time-to-offer: 13% declined due to long timeline — target under 5 days from final interview to offer. (3) Competing offers: implement exploding offer policy with 72-hour decision window for senior roles. (4) Counter-offers: brief candidates on counter-offer likelihood during offer call. (5) Role scope: align JD with actual role scope — 3 declines suggest role was mis-scoped. (6) Compensation: ensure offer is within 5% of candidate's stated expectation gathered at screening.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"offer_improvement_actions\"},\"id\":\"offer-003\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer acceptance rates by sourcing channel. Company Website converts best once an offer is made: 85.7% acceptance rate (6 of 7 offers). Glassdoor is second at 77.8% (7 of 9). AngelList and LinkedIn both sit at 50.0%. Hackathon is the weakest closer at 40.0% — candidates from events may be passively exploring. Benchmark: industry average offer acceptance is 70-75%; channels below 55% warrant re-evaluation.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"channel_acceptance\"},\"id\":\"src-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Industry benchmarks for rejection patterns in technical hiring. Typical rejection rates: Engineering roles 55-65%, Data Science 50-60%, Product 45-55%. Expected early-stage (R1) rejection rate: 35-45% — screens out unqualified applicants. Expected compensation mismatch rate: 5-10% — above 12% signals JD salary range not being shared. Background check failure rate benchmark: 2-4%; above 8% suggests insufficient pre-screening. Culture fit rejection rate: 5-10%; above 12% may signal inconsistent culture definition across panels. No-show benchmark: under 5%; above 7% suggests poor candidate engagement or scheduling friction.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"industry_rejection_benchmarks\"},\"id\":\"rej-004\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The recommendation is actionable and relevant, addressing key issues like time-to-offer and role clarity. It aligns well with the golden scenario, though it lacks specific entity mentions.

suggestions.rationale: All agents within cost, latency, and quality targets.
